title: Binary Search on Rotated Array with Repeated Elements and Index Constraint

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array of integers that has been rotated (shifted) an unknown number of times and possibly contains duplicate elements, write a function to search for a target number in the array within a given range.
statement.inputFormat: The input consists of three parts: the array `nums`, the target number `target`, and the index range `[low, high]`.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return `True` if the target number is found within the given range, otherwise return `False`.

hints.text: Consider using a modified binary search algorithm that takes into account the repeated elements in the array.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: A brute force approach can be used to check each element in the search range, but a binary search algorithm is more efficient.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: O(n*k) time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: A binary search algorithm can be used to find the target number in the array within the search range.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a modified binary search algorithm to handle the repeated elements and index constraint.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm is correct because it divides the search space in half with each iteration, and the target number is guaranteed to be present in the search range.

functionSignature.cpp: bool search(vector<int>& nums, int target, int low, int high);
functionSignature.java: boolean search(int[] nums, int target, int low, int high);
functionSignature.python: def search(self, nums: list[int], target: int, low: int, high: int) -> bool:
functionSignature.javascript: var search = function(nums, target, low, high) {
